questions.question: In supervised learning, what makes a training example supervised rather than unsupervised?

choices.A: Each example includes an input and its expected output label.
choices.B: The model automatically discovers labels from hidden patterns.
choices.C: The dataset only contains features with no prediction target.
choices.D: The algorithm never compares predictions with true answers.

questions.explanation: Supervised learning relies on labeled examples, meaning the correct target is known during training.

questions.question: For a house-price model, which variable is the target?

choices.B: The sale price of the house
choices.C: The number of rooms used as input
choices.D: The list of features after preprocessing

questions.explanation: The target is the value the model is asked to predict, here the sale price.

questions.question: Which statement best describes a feature in a supervised model?

choices.A: It is the evaluation metric used after training.
choices.B: It is an input variable used to help predict the target.
choices.C: It is the final label predicted by the model.
choices.D: It is a mistake made by the model during validation.

questions.explanation: A feature is one of the inputs the model uses to estimate the correct target.

questions.question: Why do we usually split data into training and test sets in supervised learning?

choices.A: To make the model memorize only the first half of the rows
choices.B: To reduce the number of features before training starts
choices.C: To estimate how well the model generalizes to unseen examples
choices.D: To guarantee that every model reaches the same accuracy

questions.explanation: A held-out test set gives an external check of whether the learned pattern works on new data.

questions.question: A model predicts whether an email is spam or not spam. What kind of supervised task is this?
questions.explanation: The output is a discrete label, so the task is classification.

questions.question: A model predicts tomorrow temperature as 29.4 degrees Celsius. What kind of supervised task is this?
questions.explanation: When the target is a continuous numeric value, the task is regression.

questions.question: Look at the diagram placeholder for a scatter plot with a fitted line. What is the best interpretation?

choices.A: It shows a regression model estimating a continuous relationship between inputs and outputs.
choices.B: It shows a clustering algorithm splitting points into hidden groups without labels.
choices.C: It shows a confusion matrix for binary classification.
choices.D: It shows a reinforcement agent selecting actions over time.

questions.explanation: A fitted line over labeled points is the classic beginner visualization for supervised regression.

questions.question: A diagram placeholder shows two classes separated by a boundary. What concept does it illustrate most directly?

choices.A: A decision boundary used by a classifier to separate labeled classes
choices.B: A principal component used for dimensionality reduction
choices.C: A reward curve used in reinforcement learning
choices.D: A density cluster discovered without any target labels

questions.explanation: A boundary separating two labeled classes is one of the main visual intuitions behind supervised classification.

questions.question: Why is it risky to evaluate a supervised model only on the same data used for training?

choices.A: Because the model may appear better than it really is on unseen data
choices.B: Because training data cannot contain any labels
choices.C: Because regression metrics require clustering labels first
choices.D: Because test data must always be larger than training data

questions.explanation: Testing on training data hides overfitting and inflates confidence about real-world performance.

questions.question: A beginner builds a churn model using a column created after the customer has already left. What is the main issue?

choices.A: The model is using leaked information that would not be available at prediction time
choices.C: The target changes from classification to regression
choices.D: The dataset no longer needs a training split

questions.explanation: Using post-event information creates leakage and leads to unrealistic performance estimates.
